sections.title: 📌 Pre-departure Actions
sections.content: Go to the bank to exchange enough Thai Baht → Confirm your bank card is enabled for international use → Confirm Alipay/WeChat is linked

sections.title: Recommended Items to Bring
sections.content:
• Disposable underwear (21+ pieces): Post-surgery you'll have tissue fluid and can't wash underwear yourself.
  • Lidocaine gel: Essential for pain relief during dilation.
  • Lubricant applicator: Keep extras, they're easy to lose.
  • Night pads/liquid sanitary pads: Much more comfortable than regular pads.
  • Standing mirror with light: For dilation and cleaning while in bed.
  • 25mm pump head: Attach to iodine bottle for easy dispensing.
  • Scissors: For opening delivery food, pad packages.
  • O-shaped/U-shaped cushion: 【Life-saving】Absolute must for flights and car rides to prevent wound pressure.

items.content: Simple breakfast (bread + milk), final check of passport/wallet/phone/power bank/neck pillow
Take taxi to airport, approximately 45-60 minutes
Check-in → Bag drop → Security check
Flight approximately 2h40m. Air conditioning is cold, bring a jacket
Follow Transfer signs, no need to go through immigration. Find next gate → restroom → wait
Flight approximately 3h. Note time difference: Thailand is 1 hour behind Beijing time
Follow Arrivals → Immigration check. Purpose: 'Medical treatment'. How long: 'About 6 weeks'

sections.title: ⚠️ Immigration Tips
sections.content: Stay calm, smile, keep answers concise. If language is a barrier, show your printed itinerary.

sections.title: 💧 Morning Focus
sections.content:
• Eat a good breakfast + drink lots of water: You'll need to do a urine test at the clinic!

⚠️ Before leaving, check your belongings:

  • Passport original (extremely important)
  • Psychiatric diagnosis certificate
  • Translation fee

items.content: Translator present. Doctor will ask about surgical expectations, health status, medication history. Physical examination and surgical plan discussion.
Blood test + urine test. Routine blood, coagulation, liver/kidney function, infectious disease screening
Translator will explain each item. Ask questions if you have any doubts
Two psychiatrists will interview you separately (about 30 minutes each). Confirm gender dysphoria diagnosis + surgical intent + psychological state
Chest X-ray + other routine pre-surgery tests, approximately 1 hour
All tests normal → Report to family

sections.title: 🚫 Today's Diet Rules
sections.content:
Only eat light, easily digestible food: Porridge (no meat), yogurt, jelly, juice

Absolutely avoid: Meat, fruits (with fiber), vegetables, any solid hard-to-digest food

sections.title: ⚠️ Critical Warning
sections.content: If the first bowel movement after surgery is dry, it can easily cause severe tearing or heavy bleeding. Make sure to buy fiber supplements or aloe capsules in advance!

sections.title: 🚫 06:00 Absolute fasting
sections.content: Cannot eat anything, cannot drink any water. Can rinse mouth but don't swallow. This is anesthesia safety requirement, must be strictly followed.

items.content: Brush teeth (don't swallow water), use restroom, change into loose clothing
Bring passport and small backpack downstairs, driver takes you to hospital
Follow guide to registration, use appointment and passport, measure height and weight
Nurse will perform enema to clear bowel again
Anesthesiologist confirms identity, IV anesthesia, count down from 10... then nothing
Surgery complete. May feel extremely cold/hot/numb/pain when waking from anesthesia. Doctor will visit to say surgery was successful.

sections.title: 💪 Before Surgery
sections.content: Deep breath: Inhale 4 seconds → Hold 4 seconds → Exhale 4 seconds. You're ready. This is the path you've chosen.

sections.title: 📅 Day 1 (Day 5) — Bed Rest
sections.content:
Doctor checks dressings and drainage tubes in the morning.

Today's tasks:

  • Do 10 deep breaths every hour (prevent lung complications)
  • Force yourself to drink 2-3 liters of water daily
  • Move limbs in bed (prevent blood clots)

Diet: Clear liquids (rice water, clear soup, juice)

sections.title: 📅 Day 2 (Day 6) — First time out of bed!
sections.content:
After doctor approves, get out of bed.

Steps: Sit up in bed for a few minutes → Sit on edge of bed for a few minutes → Stand with nurse's help → Walk slowly holding wall

Goal: Walk 2-3 times after meals, 5-10 minutes each

sections.title: 📅 Day 3 (Day 7) — Gradual improvement
sections.content:
Pain continues to decrease, can walk to bathroom yourself. Appetite improves.

Drainage tube may be removed. Starting to feel bored — this is a good sign!

sections.title: 📅 Day 4 (Day 8) — Packing removal
sections.content:
Most uncomfortable procedure during hospital stay: Doctor removes internal dressing.

Strong stretching sensation and stinging, about 5-10 minutes. Deep breath, try to relax.

After removal, feel much better. Doctor teaches post-surgery cleaning.

sections.title: 📅 Day 5 (Day 9) — Discharge!
sections.content:
Doctor confirms everything is normal → Sign discharge papers → Get medications and go home.

Discharge medications: Painkillers · Antibiotics (7-10 days) · Topical ointment · Stool softener

⚠️ Note: Still have catheter when discharged, still absolutely cannot shower, can only use wet wipes

sections.title: 📅 Day 17 — First Follow-up
sections.content:
Most important follow-up:

① Wound check ② Catheter removal ③ Try urinating ④ Dilation teaching

Catheter removal: Slight pulling sensation, doesn't hurt. After removal, try urinating. First time may have burning sensation.

🎉 After catheter removal, can finally take real showers!

sections.title: ⚠️ Two Iron Rules
sections.content:
1. Absolutely do not dilate after 4pm! (Clinic closes at 5pm, if something goes wrong there's no time to handle it)
2. Do not try to touch anything for 2 weeks after surgery! (Very easy to cause complications)

sections.title: 📅 Day 24 — Second Follow-up
sections.content:
Doctor checks wound → Teaches cleaning → Points out clitoris location → Evaluates dilation progress

Issues: Fit-to-fly letter + Surgery Certificate

These two documents are very important for returning home and subsequent document changes!

sections.title: 🧳 Luggage Checklist
items: Dilators — bring back home to continue using

items.content: Clinic arranges car to take you. Arrive 3 hours early recommended.
Show fit-to-fly letter at airline counter, can request free wheelchair.
Stand up and walk every 2-3 hours (prevent blood clots) · Drink lots of water · Wear loose clothing
Immigration → Collect luggage → Taxi home → Take a good shower → Eat a longed-for meal → Report safe → Good sleep

sections.title: Document Changes
sections.content:
Materials: Surgery Certificate (notarized translation) · Fit-to-fly letter · Passport · Household registration · ID card original · Hospital gender change certificate

Process: Hospital gender assessment → Get medical certificate → Bring to local police station for change → Change household registration gender & name → Re-issue ID card
